The genus Guarianthe is also part of the subfamily Epidendroideae, which includes various epiphytic orchids. intro: Guarianthe patinii is a rare and endangered species of orchid, native to Cuba, that is highly valued for its medicinal properties and unique fragrance. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Guarianthe patinii contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Guarianthe patinii is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make teas, tinctures, and salves to treat a range of health conditions.
